summary: Registered the user-provided public OS RAG learning packet as DinoBrain durable knowledge. The representative URL anchors remain anchor-only/unverified source candidates, while a new accepted memory records how future sessions should use them for LLM Wiki source/chunk/claim lineage, contextual retrieval, hybrid retrieval, reranking, canaries, and behavior evaluation.
decisions: Treat the public OS RAG material as a source-anchor and implementation backlog, not as verified source truth.
Do not invent the missing upstream 55-anchor list; only exact local URLs are registered.
Promote the durable lesson into accepted memory so future sessions know to use source/chunk/claim lineage plus retrieval canaries and behavior evaluation.
next_steps: Import the exact remaining upstream RAG URL list if available.
Fetch and chunk high-priority sources one at a time, then review concrete claims before using them as factual support.
Improve ranking so accepted knowledge and source catalogs outrank broad operational behavior rules for retrieval-intent queries.
search_queries: cross OS RAG learning packet LLM Wiki source chunk claim lineage contextual retrieval hybrid reranking canaries
rag-public-os-learning-packet cross-os-learning accepted project memory retrieval-quality backlog
